sglang.title: Always trigger pr-test
sglang.author: merrymercy
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Always trigger pr test and skip the needed tests.
This is to make the job pr-test-finish always run so it unblocks CI.

sglang.title: [sgl-kernel performace] fix fp8 quant kernels dispatch __nv_fp8_e4m3 bug to improve performance 10%-20%
sglang.author: BBuf
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Follow: #8449
Thanks to @strgrb .
To boost kernel performance, we recommend using the __nv_fp8_e4m3 data type instead of c10::Float8_e4m3fn to do float->fp8 dtype. This change reduces the number of instructions needed for data conversion, which in turn optimizes overall execution efficien

sglang.title: feat(hicache): support file backend reading directory config form env.
sglang.author: hzh0425
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Supports reading the ​file backend​'s dir configuration from environment variables. Currently, it ​defaults to reading from the /tmp directory, which may potentially ​fill up the /tmp directory.
To address this, a ​configurable approach​ is implemented, allowing users to ​flexibly specify

sglang.title: feature(pd-hicache): Prefill instances support reusing the RemoteStorage Cache via HiCache.
sglang.author: hzh0425
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
co-author: @ShangmingCai
parent issue: #8210
Motivation
In the ​previous PR​ (#7704), support for ​HICache + L3 RemoteCache​ (e.g., mooncake/3fs, etc.) was already implemented.
The ​goal of this PR​ is to enable ​KVCache reuse across Prefill instances​ via ​HICache + L3 RemoteCache​ in ​PD-Separatio

vllm.title: [Bugfix] VLLM_V1 supports passing other compilation levels
vllm.author: zou3519
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Before this PR, V1 always forced the compilation level to be 3 (PIECEWISE). In VLLM V0, users had an option to set the compilation level to be something else. This PR changes V1 to respect the compilation level set by the user.
The other compilation levels are mostly just used for debugging

vllm.title: Add `flashinfer_python` to CUDA wheel requirements
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
We have installed flashinfer by default in the docker image for a long time, but now as we use flashinfer for many critical kernels for NVIDIA Blackwell we should consider adding it to the default CUDA dependencies.
The flashinfer-python wheel by default does not include pre-compiled kernels

vllm.title: docker: docker-aware precompiled wheel support
vllm.author: dougbtv
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Main goal is in the context of CI, in order to not build wheels when unnecessary, and speed up CI builds overall.

added VLLM_DOCKER_BUILD_CONTEXT to keep precompiled wheel logic in  setup.py but add parameterization for use during a docker build.
normalized VLLM_USE_PRECOMPILED, treat only "1" or "

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Correct max tokens for non-contiguous embeds
vllm.author: milesial
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Fixes a hang on some VLM models with large images.
Some models like Pixtral derivative do not have contiguous multimodal image embeddings, but insert text tokens in between chunks of image row embeddings.
The encoder cache manager did not take this into account, and underestimated the size of the ca
